$comment: Harness-wide cost-tier -> model routing for the gamebook studio. Waves and agents read this instead of hardcoding a model. HARD RULES: (1) a write-deciding step (author, or a decision the orchestrator acts on) is never silently downgraded to a cheap tier; (2) the three project-fate verdicts (concept greenlight, slice greenlight, RC gate) route to the `greenlight` tier — the strongest model available; (3) every agent() call in a wave passes an explicit model — the Workflow tool otherwise inherits the session model (Fable) and silently runs cheap roles at premium pricing.

roles.$comment: Per-role tier. Department directors + producer are judges (they gate, rarely author). greenlight-panel is a routing alias: greenlight waves invoke a director agentType at the greenlight tier for pass/redesign/kill verdicts.

rules.no_silent_downgrade: A step that authors code/content or makes a decision the orchestrator acts on must resolve to `author` or stronger. Never route it to `verifier`/`parser` to save cost.
rules.gate_set_never_authors: qa-gate-verifier, qa-playtest-analyst, qa-compliance, qa-crash-correlator, and resolve-project must never write files; they only read and return a verdict. These are the roles the non-trusting gate depends on.
rules.greenlight_routes_up: concept-greenlight, slice-greenlight, and rc waves route their panel verdict to the `greenlight` tier (Fable). These are the three calls that can kill or advance a project; never downgrade them.
rules.explicit_model_always: Every agent() call in a wave passes model: M['<role>']. Omitting it inherits the session model — a cost bug, caught by check-wave-mirrors.mjs.
